---
title: "Per-gene boxplots and non-/parametric tests across cell lines"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# Per-gene Shapiro test followed by t-test/Wilcoxon (HPV+/-)
# and ANOVA/Kruskal (cell lines). Inputs come from PRECLIN_TX_DATA.
source(here::here("config.R"))
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

######using same analyses with vst normalization

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggpubr)
library(DESeq2)
library(FSA)
# Load data
count_data <- read.csv(file.path(PRECLIN_TX_DATA, "countall.csv"))
count_data = aggregate(count_data,FUN = max,by=list(count_data$Genes))
count_data <- count_data[,-1]
Metadata <- read.csv(file.path(PRECLIN_TX_DATA, "Metadata_HNSCC.csv"))
rownames(Metadata) <- Metadata$Sample
rownames(count_data) <- count_data$Genes
count_data <- count_data [,-1]

# Apply VST
vst_data <- varianceStabilizingTransformation(as.matrix(count_data))
vst_df <- as.data.frame(vst_data)

# Assuming vst_df has been created as shown in previous steps
Metadata_ordered <- Metadata[match(colnames(vst_df), rownames(Metadata)),]
vst_long <- cbind.data.frame(t(vst_df), Metadata_ordered)


# Combine with metadata
vst_all <- cbind(vst_long, Metadata_ordered)

# Define genes of interest
#genes <- c("TP53", "MAGEA3", "MAGEA6", "MAGEA4", "PTPRD", "NDN", "ZNF214", "SALL1", "ZPBP2")
genes <- c("EREG", "MEI1", "RIBC2", "TCAM1P", "SMC1B", "LY75", "SP9", "ZSCAN23"
   )
# Convert to long format for faceting
long_data <- vst_all %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(genes), names_to = "Gene", values_to = "Expression")
```


```{r}
analyze_and_plot <- function(gene_data, gene_name) {
  # Subset data for the current gene
  gene_df <- gene_data %>% filter(Gene == gene_name)
  
  cat("\n\n### Analysis for Gene:", gene_name, "###\n")
  
  # Shapiro test
  shapiro_res <- shapiro.test(gene_df$Expression)
  cat("\nShapiro-Wilk normality test:\n")
  print(shapiro_res)
  
  # Determine if the data is normally distributed based on Shapiro-Wilk test
  if (shapiro_res$p.value > 0.05) {
    cat("\nData is normally distributed. Performing parametric tests (ANOVA, Tukey HSD, t-test).\n")
    
    # T-test
    ttest_res <- t.test(Expression ~ HPV, data = gene_df)
    cat("\nWelch Two Sample t-test:\n")
    print(ttest_res)
    
    # Boxplot + t-test
    plot_ttest <- gene_df %>%
      ggplot(aes(x = HPV, y = Expression)) +
      geom_boxplot(aes(fill = HPV), outlier.shape = 15, outlier.colour = "black", width = 0.5, alpha = .5) +
      geom_jitter(aes(color = HPV), size = 0.6, alpha = 0.9) +
      xlab("HPV") + theme_classic() +
      stat_compare_means(method = "t.test") + 
      scale_fill_manual(values = c("#E69F00", "#56B4E9")) +
      ggtitle(paste("Gene:", gene_name))
    print(plot_ttest)
    
    # ANOVA
    anova_res <- aov(Expression ~ Cell_Line, data = gene_df)
    cat("\nANOVA results:\n")
    print(summary(anova_res))
    
    # TukeyHSD
    tukey_res <- TukeyHSD(anova_res)
    cat("\nTukeyHSD post-hoc test:\n")
    print(tukey_res)
    
    # Boxplot + ANOVA
    plot_anova <- gene_df %>%
      ggplot(aes(x = Cell_Line, y = Expression)) +
      geom_boxplot(aes(fill = HPV), outlier.shape = 15, outlier.colour = "black", width = 0.5, alpha = .5) +
      geom_jitter(aes(color = Cell_Line), size = 0.6, alpha = 0.9) +
      xlab("Cell Line") + theme_classic() +
      stat_compare_means(method = "anova") +
      scale_fill_manual(values = c("#E69F00", "#56B4E9")) +
      ggtitle(paste("Gene:", gene_name))
    print(plot_anova)
    
  } else {
    cat("\nData is not normally distributed. Performing non-parametric tests (Wilcoxon, Kruskal-Wallis, Dunn).\n")
    
    # Wilcoxon test
    wilcox_res <- wilcox.test(Expression ~ HPV, data = gene_df)
    cat("\nWilcoxon rank-sum test:\n")
    print(wilcox_res)
    
    # Boxplot + Wilcoxon test
    plot_wilcox <- gene_df %>%
      ggplot(aes(x = HPV, y = Expression)) +
      geom_boxplot(aes(fill = HPV), outlier.shape = 15, outlier.colour = "black", width = 0.5, alpha = .5) +
      geom_jitter(aes(color = HPV), size = 0.6, alpha = 0.9) +
      xlab("HPV") + theme_classic() +
      stat_compare_means(method = "wilcox.test") + 
      scale_fill_manual(values = c("#E69F00", "#56B4E9")) +
      ggtitle(paste("Gene:", gene_name))
    print(plot_wilcox)
    
    # Kruskal-Wallis test
    kruskal_res <- kruskal.test(Expression ~ Cell_Line, data = gene_df)
    cat("\nKruskal-Wallis test:\n")
    print(kruskal_res)
    
    # Dunn post-hoc test
    dunn_res <- dunnTest(Expression ~ Cell_Line, data = gene_df, method = "bh")
    cat("\nDunn post-hoc test:\n")
    print(dunn_res)
    
    # Boxplot + Kruskal-Wallis test
    plot_kruskal <- gene_df %>%
      ggplot(aes(x = Cell_Line, y = Expression)) +
      geom_boxplot(aes(fill = HPV), outlier.shape = 15, outlier.colour = "black", width = 0.5, alpha = .5) +
      geom_jitter(aes(color = Cell_Line), size = 0.6, alpha = 0.9) +
      xlab("Cell Line") + theme_classic() +
      stat_compare_means(method = "kruskal.test") +
      scale_fill_manual(values = c("#E69F00", "#56B4E9")) +
      ggtitle(paste("Gene:", gene_name))
    print(plot_kruskal)
  }
  
  # Boxplot + Wilcoxon test (treatment arm)
  plot_treatment_arm <- gene_df %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = Group, y = Expression)) +
    geom_boxplot(aes(fill = HPV), outlier.shape = 15, outlier.colour = "black", width = 0.5, alpha = .5) +
    geom_jitter(aes(color = Cell_Line), size = 0.6, alpha = 0.9) +
    xlab("HPV") + theme_classic() + 
    stat_compare_means(method = ifelse(shapiro_res$p.value > 0.05, "t.test", "wilcox.test")) + 
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("#E69F00", "#56B4E9")) +
    ggtitle(paste("Gene:", gene_name))
  print(plot_treatment_arm)
  
  return(list(
    gene_name = gene_name, 
    shapiro_res = shapiro_res, 
    ttest_res = if (exists("ttest_res")) ttest_res else NULL, 
    wilcox_res = if (exists("wilcox_res")) wilcox_res else NULL, 
    anova_res = if (exists("anova_res")) summary(anova_res) else NULL, 
    tukey_res = if (exists("tukey_res")) tukey_res else NULL, 
    kruskal_res = if (exists("kruskal_res")) kruskal_res else NULL, 
    dunn_res = if (exists("dunn_res")) dunn_res else NULL
  ))
}

```


```{r}
# Initialize an empty list to store results
results_list <- list()

# Open a PDF device
pdf(file.path(PRECLIN_TX_OUT, "comparisoncelllines_plot.pdf"), width = 8, height = 6)

# Apply function to each gene and store the results
for (gene in genes) {
  result <- analyze_and_plot(long_data, gene)
  results_list[[gene]] <- result
  
  # If the result is a ggplot object, print it to the PDF
  if (inherits(result, "ggplot")) {
    print(result)
  }
}

# Close the PDF device
dev.off()

```
